deep_dive.title: A Practical Investigation of Training-free Relaxed Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates sampling from an autoregressive LLM by using a faster auxiliary model to draft tokens which are then verified in parallel by the LLM. Standard speculative decoding is lossless: its rejection and resampling steps exactly preserve the LLM's sampling distribution. Recent work argues that relaxing this strict guarantee can yield further speed-ups, controlled capability-speed trade-offs, or even capability gains. We practically investigate training-free relaxed speculative decoding techniques, unify existing approaches within a shared framework, benchmark them on contemporary settings, and distil takeaways and empirical findings for practitioners. Important takeaways include: relaxation can require considerable capability evaluation unlike lossless speculative decoding, and many relaxed approaches rely on a drafter that is a good language model, making them unsuited for lightweight dedicated multi-token-prediction drafters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free relaxed speculative decoding for LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM的无训练宽松投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向LLM的无训练宽松投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM的无训练宽松投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A practical investigation of training-free relaxed speculative decoding techniques, unifying existing approaches and benchmarking on contemporary settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文对无训练宽松投机解码技术进行了实践性研究，将现有方法统一在一个框架下，并在当前主流设置上进行基准测试。研究发现，放松严格保证可以在可控的能力-速度权衡下获得额外加速，但需要谨慎选择松弛策略。实验结果提炼了实用建议，帮助实践者更好地应用松弛投机解码。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文对无训练宽松投机解码技术进行了实践性研究，将现有方法统一在一个框架下，并在当前主流设置上进行基准测试。研究发现，放松严格保证可以在可控的能力-速度权衡下获得额外加速，但需要谨慎选择松弛策略。实验结果提炼了实用建议，帮助实践者更好地应用松弛投机解码。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

deep_dive.title: Unlocking Parallelism in Autoregressive Language Models via Speculative Decoding with Progressive Tree Drafting
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding has significantly accelerated Large Language Model (LLM) inference by alleviating memory-bound bottlenecks. However, traditional speculative decoding typically relies on auxiliary draft modules, incurring significant training and communication overhead. Although recent methods attempt to generate drafts within the target model itself, they often fail to fully exploit its latent parallel capacity due to a lack of structural coordination. In this paper, we propose \textbf{Progressive Tree Drafting (PTD)}, which employs a structured, guided parallel drafting strategy to harness the model's parallel potential. By coupling a progressive tree structure with a stepwise pruning mechanism, PTD actively guides the LLM to explore multiple semantic paths in a single forward pass, ensuring both draft diversity and coherence. Experiments demonstrate that PTD achieves up to $2\times$ decoding speedup across various benchmarks while remaining training-free and model-agnostic. Our code is available at: https://github.com/MINE-USTC/PTD.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes Progressive Tree Drafting for speculative decoding to accelerate LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出渐进树束草稿的投机解码方法，加速LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出渐进树束草稿的投机解码方法，加速LLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出渐进树束草稿的投机解码方法，加速LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Progressive Tree Drafting (PTD), a structured parallel drafting strategy for speculative decoding that accelerates autoregressive LLM inference without auxiliary draft modules.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码是加速大语言模型推理的有效方法，但现有方法依赖辅助草稿模块或未能充分利用模型并行潜力。本文提出渐进树束草稿（PTD），通过渐进树结构和逐步剪枝机制，在目标模型内部实现结构化并行草稿生成。实验表明，PTD在不引入额外训练开销的情况下显著提升解码速度，为LLM高效推理提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 投机解码是加速大语言模型推理的有效方法，但现有方法依赖辅助草稿模块或未能充分利用模型并行潜力。本文提出渐进树束草稿（PTD），通过渐进树结构和逐步剪枝机制，在目标模型内部实现结构化并行草稿生成。实验表明，PTD在不引入额外训练开销的情况下显著提升解码速度，为LLM高效推理提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.

deep_dive.title: D-cut: Adaptive Verification Depth Pruning for Batched Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates large language model (LLM) inference without compromising output quality. Recent parallel drafting methods further improve single-request performance by decoupling draft length from drafting latency, enabling longer drafts and higher mean accepted tokens (MAT). However, under high request concurrency, long drafts waste substantial computation on rejected tokens, increasing verification cost and potentially making speculative decoding slower than autoregressive decoding. We present D-Cut, an adaptive pruning method that selects draft tokens jointly across the batch and concentrates the verification budget on tokens most likely to be accepted. D-Cut is motivated by two observations. First, acceptance lengths vary considerably across concurrent requests; D-Cut therefore performs cross-request pruning, allocating the verification budget adaptively according to draft confidence. Second, verification cost depends strongly on the deployment environment, including GPU architecture and parallelism strategy; D-Cut incorporates a runtime cost model to adapt its pruning depth to the target environment. Experiments on dense and mixture-of-experts (MoE) models show that, under high concurrency, D-Cut improves the average speedup from \(1.26\times\) to \(1.65\times\), restores acceleration in dense-model configurations where long-draft baselines are slower than autoregressive decoding, and achieves up to \(3.0\times\) speedup over autoregressive decoding on MoE models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adaptive verification depth pruning for batched speculative decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向批处理投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向批处理投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向批处理投机解码的自适应验证深度剪枝
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: D-Cut adaptively prunes verification depth across batches in speculative decoding, reducing wasted computation on rejected tokens and improving throughput.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对高并发场景下投机解码中长草稿浪费计算的问题，D-Cut方法提出自适应验证深度剪枝，联合跨批次选择最可能被接受的令牌，集中验证预算。实验表明，该方法在保持输出质量的同时显著提高了吞吐量，甚至在某些情况下快于自回归解码。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对高并发场景下投机解码中长草稿浪费计算的问题，D-Cut方法提出自适应验证深度剪枝，联合跨批次选择最可能被接受的令牌，集中验证预算。实验表明，该方法在保持输出质量的同时显著提高了吞吐量，甚至在某些情况下快于自回归解码。

quick_skim.title: Seeing the End at Step Zero: Accelerating Diffusion MLLMs via MLP Sparsity-Aware Truncation
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Multimodal Large Language Models (DMLLMs) are highly effective for multimodal reasoning, yet their inference efficiency is significantly hindered by fixed-length generation constraints. Since the actual output length is unknown, output sequences are padded to a predefined maximum length, resulting in substantial redundant computation over unnecessary [EOS] tokens. In this work, we discover that DMLLMs implicitly reveal their valid semantic boundary at the very first denoising step through a distinct shift in MLP activation sparsity. Leveraging this observation, we propose Seer, a training-free framework that detects this boundary using a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR)-based criterion and performs one-shot truncation of the redundant suffix for all subsequent computations. To preserve these theoretical gains during batched serving, Seer incorporates a hybrid execution strategy that maximizes throughput while seamlessly accommodating dynamic sequence lengths. Experimental results demonstrate that Seer effectively eliminates padding waste, accelerating throughput by up to $\sim$31$\times$. Across 9 benchmarks, Seer robustly maintains overall performance and even improves accuracy on complex visual tasks by mitigating noise leakage (e.g., DocVQA score increases from 63.52 to 63.66), offering a highly efficient, plug-and-play solution for DMLLM acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: accelerates DMLLM inference via MLP sparsity truncation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过MLP稀疏性截断加速扩散多模态大模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过MLP稀疏性截断加速扩散多模态大模型推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过MLP稀疏性截断加速扩散多模态大模型推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Seer accelerates Diffusion Multimodal LLMs by truncating unnecessary tokens at the first denoising step using SNR-based MLP sparsity detection, reducing computation without quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散多模态大语言模型（DMLLMs）的推理受固定长度生成的冗余计算严重拖累。本文发现模型在第一步去噪时通过MLP激活稀疏性隐含揭示有效语义边界，并提出Seer框架，利用信号噪声比标准检测边界并一次性截断冗余令牌。实验表明该方法在几乎不损失质量的情况下显著加速推理，为多模态模型的高效部署提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散多模态大语言模型（DMLLMs）的推理受固定长度生成的冗余计算严重拖累。本文发现模型在第一步去噪时通过MLP激活稀疏性隐含揭示有效语义边界，并提出Seer框架，利用信号噪声比标准检测边界并一次性截断冗余令牌。实验表明该方法在几乎不损失质量的情况下显著加速推理，为多模态模型的高效部署提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Reflex: Real-Time VLA Control through Streaming Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Flow matching Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models promise precise continuous control, but their iterative denoising nature introduces fundamental incompatibilities with real-time robotics: global timestep injection invalidates KV-caching, forcing a choice between slow $O(N^2)$ re-computation or mathematically incorrect cache reuse. We present \textbf{Reflex}, a framework that enables \textit{real-time streaming inference} for flow matching policies by exploiting the \textit{Timestep-Invariance Property} -- that perception encoders are functionally independent of the denoising loop. Reflex partitions the attention context into static, sliding, and dynamic regions, enabling $O(1)$ incremental cache updates while preserving full-batch-equivalent attention outputs for fixed inputs. To ensure stability under continuous high-frequency inference, we introduce \textit{AdaRMSNorm}, an adaptive normalization layer that prevents BFloat16 numerical collapse by gating on flow phase. We further maximize throughput through an \textit{async pipeline} that decouples visual encoding from action generation, combined with \textit{operator fusion} that reduces kernel overhead. On LIBERO and Kinetix benchmarks, Reflex achieves a 2.58$\times$ inference speedup and 50Hz stable streaming, reducing reaction latency by up to 54\% and enabling efficient deployment without performance degradation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: streaming inference for VLA models with KV-cache optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向VLA模型的流式推理，包含KV缓存优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向VLA模型的流式推理，包含KV缓存优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向VLA模型的流式推理，包含KV缓存优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reflex enables real-time streaming inference for flow matching VLA policies by leveraging timestep-invariance and incremental cache updates, achieving O(1) attention context management.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Reflex框架，针对流匹配VLA模型推理慢的问题，利用时间步不变性将感知编码器与去噪循环解耦，通过将注意力上下文划分为静态、滑动和动态区域，实现O(1)增量缓存更新，在不牺牲精度的情况下支持实时流式推理。实验表明该方法可有效加速VLA模型在实际机器人控制中的推理速度。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出Reflex框架，针对流匹配VLA模型推理慢的问题，利用时间步不变性将感知编码器与去噪循环解耦，通过将注意力上下文划分为静态、滑动和动态区域，实现O(1)增量缓存更新，在不牺牲精度的情况下支持实时流式推理。实验表明该方法可有效加速VLA模型在实际机器人控制中的推理速度。
